subject: Sociology
title: Religion as Conservative Force
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Religion as Conservative Force.

hook.text: For centuries, kings ruled by divine right. Questioning the monarch meant questioning God himself.

buildup.text: Functionalists and Marxists agree on one thing: religion often upholds the existing social order.

discovery.text: By teaching acceptance and promising afterlife rewards, religion can discourage challenges to authority.

twist.text: This isn't always intentional. Communities cling to tradition because it provides comfort and stability.

climax.text: Religion as a conservative force doesn't mean it's wrong. It means it tends to slow social change.

punchline.text: Faith often favors the status quo.

quiz.question: How can religion act as a conservative force?
options: By teaching acceptance of the current order
